content.claim: Germany's East German states replaced comprehensive primary schools (POS) with early three-tier tracking after reunification, leading to a sharp rise in educational inequality; most German Länder subsequently reformed tracking into two-tier models but retained the prestige position of the Gymnasium
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: East German case reflects transition from centrally-planned to market system, not directly comparable to Hungary's context
German reforms did not eliminate tracking, only restructured it
Does not isolate tracking reform effects from other concurrent institutional changes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
